scope: K group round 2 betting risk observation; no executable betting advice.

source_log.context: K group round 2 threads dispatched; expected outputs listed

observation_items: Portugal may carry public-team and star-name premium after a first-round draw with DR Congo.
Uzbekistan's 1-3 first-round loss can create both market discount and tactical urgency; without model probabilities, no market side can be upgraded.
risk_points: favorite tax on Portugal if market prices reputation rather than first-round conversion issues
Uzbekistan forced-response game state could raise totals volatility
waiting_items: latest injuries, suspensions, and minutes limits
official Jingcai odds and same-source 1X2/handicap/totals snapshot
avoid_items: any Portugal handicap or scoreline stance before model and market snapshot
any totals stance without lineup and same-source totals odds
failure_conditions: Portugal rotates or key attackers are minutes-limited
Uzbekistan starts a more aggressive transition unit
market shortens Portugal without team-news support

observation_items: Colombia's 3-1 first-round win can create recency premium.
DR Congo's draw with Portugal keeps physical and defensive-resistance paths live; without model probabilities, Colombia cannot be treated as a low-risk leg.
risk_points: recency bias after Colombia's first-round win
draw suppression if market underprices DR Congo's resistance
set-piece and transition volatility unknown until data refresh
waiting_items: official Jingcai odds and same-source market snapshot
avoid_items: any Colombia single-side upgrade before red-team pass
any combo construction before official odds
failure_conditions: Colombia lineup rotation or midfield control downgrade
DR Congo starts a high-duel, set-piece-heavy lineup
market overreacts to Colombia's 3-1 first-round result

no_bet_option: Do not buy / wait for official lineups and odds.
handoff_notes: Re-run this risk observation after model, red team, and data refresh files land.
Status must remain hold_not_executable until all gates clear and red-team verdict is pass.
